checks.name: val time >= train time (per user)
checks.detail: 0 strict violations; 26,088/162,541 users have a timestamp tie (bulk-rated multiple items at the same second). Split still correct via stable-sort ordering.

checks.name: test time >= val time (per user)
checks.detail: 0 strict violations; 25,671/162,541 users have a timestamp tie between val and test.

checks.name: (user, item) appears in at most one split
checks.detail: no (user, item) pair appears in multiple splits

checks.name: val and test rated within same session
checks.detail: 144,893/162,541 users (89.1%) rated val and test within 3600s. High % means XGB val-training and test eval are highly correlated; ranker numbers look better than for temporally separated targets.

checks.name: ALS factor shapes match dense indices
popularity counts derived only from train
checks.detail: 100 random items match train counts exactly

checks.name: cooc derived only from train
checks.detail: 100 random nonzero pairs both have train positives

checks.name: XGB training positives are val items (post-fix)
checks.detail: 94,476 users have a val positive available as ranker training target. Reference: src/ranker_xgb.py uses `val_target_per_user` for training positives.

checks.name: test target NOT in user's train history
checks.detail: checked 162,541 test items; none in any user's train history

checks.name: test target ≠ val target per user
checks.detail: checked 162,541 users with both val & test; all distinct items

checks.name: personalized retrieval reaches more catalog than popularity
XGB-train users overlap with eval-test users (same user, different items)
checks.detail: 66,548 users are in BOTH the XGB val-positive pool and the eval test pool. Same user, different held-out items — no item-level leakage. A stricter cohort-split would partition users disjointly.

checks.name: sampled-99-negatives is known biased (Krichene & Rendle 2020)
checks.detail: Uniformly sampled negatives are dominantly long-tail garbage; metric is biased upward for all methods. Trust the full-catalog protocol for production-realism; use sampled only for literature comparison.

checks.name: single seed, no multi-run stability
checks.detail: All randomness seeded to 42. No confidence intervals across seeds.

checks.name: users with fewer than min_user_ratings excluded
checks.detail: min_user_ratings=5; 162,541 users retained. Cold-start users are not represented — results don't generalize to that cohort.

checks.name: positive-rating threshold is heuristic
checks.detail: rating >= 4.0 treated as positive. Optimal threshold may depend on data; not tuned.
